title: Planning Enforcement Investigation Timelines
body:
Westminster Local Enforcement Plan — Investigation Timelines:

1. URGENT (same day/next working day site visit): Works to listed buildings, demolition in conservation areas, works that are causing or likely to cause irreversible harm.

2. HIGH PRIORITY (site visit within 5 working days): Unauthorised development in conservation areas, significant operational development without permission, material change of use causing residential amenity harm, loss of residential accommodation.

3. STANDARD (site visit within 15 working days): Breach of conditions, minor operational development, advertisements, changes of use with limited harm.

4. LOW PRIORITY (site visit within 20 working days): Boundary disputes, householder alterations outside conservation areas, minor advertisement breaches.

All complaints must be acknowledged within 3 working days.
Initial assessment decision must be communicated within 8 weeks.
Planning Contravention Notices: response required within 21 days.
Temporary Stop Notices: effective for 28 days from service.
Enforcement Notices: minimum 28 days before taking effect.
Breach of Condition Notices: no right of appeal; immediate compliance required.

title: Conservation Area and Heritage Enforcement
body:
Westminster Heritage Enforcement Policy:

1. LISTED BUILDINGS: Unauthorised works to a listed building constitute a CRIMINAL OFFENCE under Section 9 of the Planning (Listed Buildings and Conservation Areas) Act 1990. Penalty: unlimited fine and/or up to 2 years imprisonment.

- Any report of works to a listed building without consent: site visit same day or next working day.
- If criminal offence confirmed: (a) serve Temporary Stop Notice immediately, (b) notify police within 24 hours, (c) prepare prosecution file within 14 working days.
- Listed Building Enforcement Notice: can require reinstatement of removed features. Compliance period typically 3-6 months.

2. CONSERVATION AREAS (Westminster has 56 conservation areas):
- Permitted development rights are restricted in conservation areas.
- Demolition requires conservation area consent.
- Material alterations visible from the highway require planning permission.
- Rear extensions exceeding single storey require permission.

3. PROSECUTION ESCALATION:
- If works continue after stop notice: immediate prosecution referral.
- Prosecution file must be submitted to legal within 14 working days of decision to prosecute.
- Westminster prioritises heritage enforcement — cases involving irreversible loss of historic fabric are treated as critical.

title: Change of Use Enforcement
body:
Westminster Change of Use Enforcement:

1. RESIDENTIAL PROTECTION: The loss of permanent residential accommodation to any other use (short-term let, office, storage) is Westminster's highest enforcement priority. Enforcement notice to be served within 4 weeks of breach confirmation.

2. COMMERCIAL CHANGE OF USE: Unauthorised changes (e.g., retail to restaurant, office to residential) assessed against harm to residential amenity.
- If causing noise, odour, or late-night disturbance: HIGH PRIORITY — enforcement notice within 6 weeks.
- PCN should be issued within 10 working days of site visit.
- If PCN not responded to within 21 days: proceed directly to enforcement notice.

3. COMPLIANCE MONITORING:
- Enforcement notice compliance to be monitored with site visit within 7 days of compliance deadline.
- Non-compliance after enforcement notice: prosecution proceedings to commence within 14 days.

4. CROSS-REFERRALS:
- Licensing breaches: refer to licensing within 5 working days.
- Environmental Health (noise, smoke, odour): refer within 3 working days.
- Health Act 2006 (indoor smoking): immediate referral.

title: Breach of Planning Conditions Enforcement
body:
Westminster Breach of Conditions Policy:

1. ASSESSMENT: Not all condition breaches justify enforcement action. Assess harm caused by the breach against the purpose of the condition.

2. BREACH OF CONDITION NOTICE (BCN):
- BCNs have NO RIGHT OF APPEAL.
- Compliance is required immediately or within the period specified.
- Use for: operating hours, noise conditions, external seating/furniture, delivery times, use restrictions.
- If non-compliance after BCN: prosecution. Fixed penalty or prosecution in Magistrates Court.

3. CONDITIONS REQUIRING SPECIALIST ASSESSMENT:
- Noise conditions (extraction, plant, music): require independent acoustic assessment. Commission within 10 working days of complaint. Report due within 20 working days.
- Odour conditions: Environmental Health assessment within 5 working days.
- Landscaping/boundary conditions: site visit to verify within 15 working days.

4. MONITORING:
- New permissions with sensitive conditions: proactive monitoring visit within 3 months of occupation/use commencing.
- Repeated breach after BCN: escalate to injunction application if prosecution insufficient.

title: Councillor and Public Enquiry Response
body:
Westminster Member and Public Enquiry Protocol:

1. COUNCILLOR ENQUIRIES: Written response within 5 working days. If the case involves ongoing enforcement, provide: current case status, next enforcement step planned, expected timeline for resolution.

2. PUBLIC COMPLAINTS: Acknowledge within 3 working days. Substantive update at 8-week point.

3. RESIDENTS ASSOCIATION / AMENITY GROUP enquiries: Treat as councillor-equivalent priority.

4. MP ENQUIRIES: Response within 3 working days.

5. PRESS ENQUIRIES: Refer to communications team immediately. Do not provide case details to press without comms approval.

6. When 3+ separate complaints received about the same breach: automatic escalation to senior enforcement officer review.
